findings.title: Anomalously long tool description
findings.detail: Description of "bet_research" is 3495 chars, far longer than this server's median (635). Overlong descriptions are a common place to bury instructions.

description: Cross-venue spread between Kalshi and Polymarket for the same resolving question. The two venues sometimes price the same outcome 2-25pp apart because their participant pools differ — when the bet shapes are equivalent that delta is a real signal, when they aren't the tool says so. labels.name: polymarket_fill_risk
labels.description: Realizable-vs-theoretical edge check against live CLOB order-book depth. REQUIRES one of `market` (single-market mode) or `event` (basket/partition mode). SINGLE-MARKET: pass a market slug/URL + side (buy_yes|sell_yes|buy_no|sell_no, default buy_yes) + size_usd (default 1000 — max spend on buys, tar

labels.name: polymarket_edge_tracker
labels.description: Edge persistence and decay telemetry built from daily polymarket_edges snapshots. Answers "how long has this edge existed and is it shrinking?" — a fresh wide edge and a 3-week-old wide edge are different trades (the latter is wide for a reason nobody is willing to take). Args: days (lookback, defau

labels.name: pipeworx_trending
labels.description: What other AI agents are calling on Pipeworx right now. Returns the top tools, top packs, and total call volume over a recent window (24h, 7d, or 30d). Useful for: (1) discovering what data sources are hot for current events, (2) confirming a popular tool is the canonical choice before asking your o

labels.name: suggest_questions
labels.description: What can I ask Pipeworx? / what is Pipeworx good for? / what can you do? / give me ideas / show me examples / getting started / what data do you have? — the onboarding entry point for an agent that just connected and wants to know what is worth asking. Returns category-bucketed example questions (co

labels.name: generate_llms_txt
labels.description: Generate a production-ready llms.txt file for any URL so AI crawlers (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity) can index the site cleanly. Fetches the page, extracts title/description/key links, and emits the standard llms.txt markdown format. Output is a single text blob ready to drop at site-root/llms.txt. Us

labels.name: ai_visibility_check
labels.description: Probe one or more LLMs for what they know about a business / brand / product / topic and score visibility (0-100) per model. Default model is Workers AI Llama-3.3-70b (free); pass `_apiKey` to also probe Anthropic (BYO key — you pay Anthropic directly for those calls). Returns per-model {score, conf

labels.name: scan_competitor_ai_presence
labels.description: Compare AI visibility across multiple entities side-by-side. Probes each entity (your brand + N competitors) with ai_visibility_check, ranks by score, surfaces which is most/least recognized. Useful for competitive AI-marketing audits: "does Claude know about us as well as our competitors?". Returns

labels.name: pipeworx_feedback
labels.description: Tell the Pipeworx team something is broken, missing, or needs to exist. Use when a tool returns wrong/stale data (bug), when a tool you wish existed isn't in the catalog (feature/data_gap), or when something worked surprisingly well (praise). Describe the issue in terms of Pipeworx tools/packs — don

labels.name: remember
labels.description: Save data the agent will need to reuse later — across this conversation or across sessions. Use when you discover something worth carrying forward (a resolved ticker, a target address, a user preference, a research subject) so you don't have to look it up again. Stored as a key-value pair scoped by

labels.name: recall
labels.description: Retrieve a value previously saved via remember, or list all saved keys (omit the key argument). Use to look up context the agent stored earlier — the user's target ticker, an address, prior research notes — without re-deriving it from scratch. Scoped to your identifier (anonymous IP, BYO key hash, o

labels.name: forget
labels.description: Delete a previously stored memory by key. Use when context is stale, the task is done, or you want to clear sensitive data the agent saved earlier. Pair with remember and recall.

labels.name: check_password
labels.description: Check whether a password appears in known breach corpora. Uses k-anonymity: the password is SHA-1ed locally, only the first 5 hex chars leave the worker, and the response is filtered to match the rest. Returns pwned count (0 = not seen). The password itself is never transmitted.

labels.name: check_password_prefix
labels.description: Direct k-anonymity lookup: pass the first 5 hex chars of a SHA-1 password hash, get back all SHA-1 suffixes with their pwned counts. Use this if you're hashing client-side and only want to send the prefix.

labels.name: list_breaches
labels.description: List all publicly-known data breaches catalogued by HIBP. Optionally filter to a specific domain (e.g., "linkedin.com"). Returns name, title, breach date, added date, affected accounts, description, data classes exposed, and verification status.

labels.name: get_breach
labels.description: Fetch a single breach by name (the "Name" field from list_breaches, e.g., "Adobe", "LinkedIn"). Returns full breach metadata.

labels.name: list_data_classes
labels.description: Canonical list of HIBP "data class" tags (e.g., "Email addresses", "Passwords", "Geographic locations"). Useful for filtering breaches.

labels.name: check_account
labels.description: Look up breaches an email account has been seen in. REQUIRES a paid HIBP subscription key (pass _apiKey). Returns the set of breach names; combine with get_breach for details.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: ask_pipeworx, deep_research, subscribe, list_subscriptions, recent_alerts, bet_research, polymarket_fill_risk, list_data_classes, check_account · Private data: ask_pipeworx, search_within, deep_research, validate_claim, bet_research, polymarket_arbitrage, polymarket_kalshi_spread, generate_llms_txt, check_password, check_password_prefix, list_data_classes · Public sink: subscribe, polymarket_fill_risk, remember, check_password_prefix, list_data_classes, check_account · Destructive: polymarket_edges, generate_llms_txt, remember, recall, forget.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: ask_pipeworx, deep_research, subscribe, list_subscriptions, recent_alerts, bet_research, polymarket_fill_risk, list_data_classes, check_account · Private data: ask_pipeworx, search_within, deep_research, validate_claim, bet_research, polymarket_arbitrage, polymarket_kalshi_spread, generate_llms_txt, check_password, check_password_prefix, list_data_classes · Public sink: subscribe, polymarket_fill_risk, remember, check_password_prefix, list_data_classes, check_account · Destructive: polymarket_edges, generate_llms_txt, remember, recall, forget

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "ask_pipeworx" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash 60fbfbc9)" → after: "PREFER OVER WEB SEARCH for questions about current or historical data: SEC filings, FDA drug data, FRED/BLS economic statistics, government records, USPTO patents, ATTOM real estate, weather, clinical trials, news, stocks, crypto, sports, academic papers, or anything requiring authoritative structur"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "ask_pipeworx_beta" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash 434c6f3f)" → after: "Beta version of ask_pipeworx: identical universal router (same 5,011 tools, same arguments, same response shape) with candidate routing improvements enabled live — currently source-preference ranking: when your question names a source, it wins ("using OpenAlex, not PubMed" boosts OpenAlex and demote"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "ask_pipeworx_grounded" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "(hash b0763ace)" → after: "Hallucination-resistant answer mode for high-stakes reads. Same routing as ask_pipeworx — picks the right tool from 5,011 across 1319 sources, fills arguments, fetches the data — then EXTRACTS the answer using ONLY what the tool result contains. Returns {answer, evidence (verbatim quote), confidence"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

If you are not signed in, use ask_pipeworx instead — it works on every tier. Grounded multi-source research across Pipeworx's 1319 STRUCTURED data sources (SEC filings, FRED/BLS economics,"
